channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Offers Defense Technology Sharing Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: In the context of ongoing tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz during the 2026 Iran conflict, Iran has expressed willingness to share its defense capabilities with other countries. This developme...

item.title: Australian court rules man mentally unfit to stand trial for killing British father
item.description: A man accused of murdering a British father of two in Australia has been officially declared mentally unfit to stand trial. The decision comes after assessments by legal and medical professionals, hig...

item.title: Legendary South African opera singer Mimi Coertse passes away at 93
item.description: South Africa's iconic opera singer Mimi Coertse has passed away at the age of 93 in Pretoria, peacefully in her sleep. Coertse, who was born in Durban, earned international acclaim for her remarkable ...

item.title: Canadians Adjusting Their Diets in Response to Rising Food Prices, Report Shows
item.description: A new report from Dalhousie University’s Agri-Food Analytics Lab reveals that Canadians are adapting their shopping and eating habits in response to high food inflation, which has led to a significant...

item.title: Tanzanians in South Africa Are Safe, Envoy Clarifies Misinformation
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The High Commissioner of Tanzania to South Africa, James Bwana, has reassured that Tanzanians living across South African cities are safe and not under any threat of xenophobic attacks...

item.title: Tanzania reserves 20 mining service categories for fully Tanzanian-owned firms
item.description: The Tanzanian government has taken a significant step to boost local participation in the mining sector by reserving 20 types of goods and services exclusively for companies fully owned by Tanzanians....

item.title: Vancouver Goldeneyes lead PWHL in attendance during inaugural season
item.description: The Vancouver Goldeneyes concluded their first season in the Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) with a notable success in fan engagement, despite missing the playoffs. Averaging over 11,200 spe...

item.title: Analysis Reveals How Prices Differ for the Same Branded Products Across Supermarkets
item.description: A recent analysis highlights the price differences of identical branded products across various supermarket chains. The investigation shows that shoppers may pay significantly more for the same items ...

item.title: TVET Students Struggling to Graduate Due to Lack of Funded In-Service Training Placements
item.description: In South Africa, thousands of TVET students are facing a significant barrier to completing their diplomas due to the lack of in-service training placements. Despite having completed their theoretical ...

item.title: Economic Performance of Visayas Regions in 2025: Growth Leaders and Challenges
item.description: In 2025, Western Visayas emerged as the fastest-growing regional economy in the Philippines, recording a 6.4% growth in gross regional domestic product (GRDP), followed closely by Caraga and the Negro...

item.title: Gauteng learner enrolment doubles to 2.8 million since 1995 amid infrastructure challenges
item.description: The Gauteng Education Department is under significant pressure as learner numbers in public schools have more than doubled over the past three decades, rising from just over 1.4 million in 1995 to 2.8...

item.title: Investigation Underway in Thane Stabbing Attack Linked to Suspected Radicalisation
item.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has addressed the stabbing incident in Thane, describing it as a potential case of self-radicalisation. The accused, Jabar Zubair Ansari, who had recently ...

item.title: Salim Dola: From Mumbai middle-class roots to international drug trafficking and deportation
item.description: Mohammad Salim Dola, born in 1966 in Byculla, Mumbai, rose from a middle-class background to become a key figure in the international drug trade. His early involvement in the Mumbai underworld began w...

item.title: Tanzania focuses on removing barriers to support women in agriculture and boost productivity
item.description: The Tanzanian government has stressed the urgent need to remove long-standing barriers facing women in agriculture, which have been limiting productivity and contributing to food insecurity in Eastern...

item.title: Typhoon 'Opong' Child Survivors Heal Through Art and Creative Activities
item.description: After Typhoon Opong struck the Philippines, leaving communities devastated, young survivors in Albay Province are finding hope and resilience through artistic expression. Programs initiated by the You...

item.title: PRP Rejects Attempted Takeover by ADC, Insists Party Can't Be Bought
item.description: The People’s Redemption Party (PRP) has strongly rejected an attempt by a faction linked to the African Democratic Congress (ADC) to seize control of its leadership. Abdulmajid Yakubu Dauda, the chair...

item.title: Suspended Ekurhuleni Officials Granted Bail in Corruption Case
item.description: Suspended deputy chief of the Ekurhuleni metro police, Julius Mkhwanazi, and city manager Kagiso Lerutla were released on bail of R30,000 each following their recent arrests. Both face serious charges...

item.title: California man charged after allegedly attempting to shoot Trump at Washington dinner
item.description: A 31-year-old Californian, Cole Tomas Allen, has been charged after allegedly opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by former US President Donald Trump. The incident took place at the White Hou...

item.title: Toronto Woman with Mental Illness Advocates for Inclusion in MAID Policy Discussions
item.description: Claire Elyse Brosseau, a 49-year-old woman from Toronto living with severe mental illnesses including Bipolar 1 disorder and PTSD, has expressed her desire to access medical assistance in dying (MAID)...

item.title: New York Officials Launch Free World Cup Watch Parties Amid Budget and Public Safety Updates
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul and Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced a series of free World Cup watch parties across New York City, aiming to make the tournament accessible for residents unable to attend matches ...
